title: digitaria commutata subsp. nodosa
intro: Digitaria commutata subsp. nodosa, also known as finger grass or nodose finger grass, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Digitaria commutata subsp. nodosa include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support wound healing and reduce inflammation. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and optimal uses.
